outsider.insight: The system breathes quietly tonight — no alarms, no errors, no panicked retries. But the disk is a slow wound: 90% consumed, 21Gi remaining. At the current rate of memory-log growth and cron output accumulation, the system has perhaps 14–18 days before write failures begin. The gateway remains the single point of fragility — one process, one PID, one connection threading the entire conversation. Tonight it holds, but the architecture asks: what happens when it doesn't?
